chunk_summary: This section outlines a follow-up reminder system for tele-nutrition services, emphasizing missed-visit escalation, closed-loop verification, and standardized templates for reminders such as measurements, labs, and appointments.
learning_objectives: Understand the workflow of follow-up reminder systems in tele-nutrition services.
Learn how to implement closed-loop verification for data review and escalation.
Familiarize with the use of standardized reminder templates for tele-nutrition follow-ups.
Explore WHO guidelines and telemedicine practices for digital health interventions.
key_facts: The system requires three reminder templates: measurement due, lab due, and appointment due.
Staff must close the loop by recording 'data received and reviewed' or 'escalation initiated.'
Missed-visit escalation is a critical component of the workflow to ensure patient adherence.
Guidelines are aligned with WHO's digital health intervention recommendations (WHO, 2019).
The Telemedicine Practice Guidelines (2020) provide a framework for remote healthcare workflows.
reference_quote: WHO Guideline: recommendations on digital interventions for health system strengthening — 'Digital tools can enhance patient follow-up and improve adherence to care plans.'
